page_content:
BMGT366 Growth Strategies for Emerging Companies (3 Credits) Offers practical management tools that are needed to build a new venture into a signiﬁcant enterprise. The competencies, strategies and structures of successful high performance businesses are studied through cases, videos and guest lecturers. Topics include leadership, internal growth strategies, merger, acquisition and franchising. Prerequisite: BMGT461 or BMGT361. Restriction: Junior standing or higher.

BMGT370 Introduction to Transportation (3 Credits) An overview of the transportation ﬁeld with an emphasis on freight movements from the perspective of both providers of capacity and users of freight services. Examines the characteristics of the freight modes and the role of each mode as a major component of logistics and supply chain management. Explores the economics, energy use, and ﬁnances of each mode as well as the impact of government policies on each mode's future. Discussion of infrastructure and capacity needs of the transportation system and its ability to support the economy.
